# Agentic Forgejo Project Template v6.7

Canonical flow:
Read Tooling Model -> Create Project Folder -> Set Forgejo -> FreeLLMAPI Deploy/Verify -> Setup Free Models -> VS Code -> OpenCode -> load .env -> GitNexus -> verify GitNexus MCP -> Caveman Code -> Paid/Frontier OpenCode Models -> Fill Required Project Documents -> Plan first task.

Core rules:
- GitNexus is required as the memory/index layer.
- OpenCode must be launched through scripts/start-opencode.* so .env is loaded.
- TAB changes agents; /models changes model route.
- Default OpenCode route is DeepSeek main model and FreeLLMAPI small model.
- OpenAI API is backup/emergency only when explicitly selected and funded.
- Caveman Code is a separate terminal coding endpoint.
- Caveman Skill is optional for agent-specific brevity behavior.

Important docs:
- docs/TOOLING_MODEL.md
- docs/HOW_TO_USE.md
- docs/OPENCODE.md
- docs/GITNEXUS.md
- docs/CAVEMAN.md
- docs/CODEX.md
- docs/FREELLMAPI.md
- docs/STATUS.md
- docs/TASKS.md
- memory/PROJECT_BRIEF.md
- memory/CONSTRAINTS.md


Endpoint reminder: VS Code is the editor; OpenCode is the configurable agent with FreeLLMAPI/DeepSeek/OpenAI routes; Caveman Code is the separate low-token terminal/Codex-capable endpoint; Codex in VS Code is optional.

- docs/IMPLEMENTATION_WORKFLOW.md: OpenCode planning to Caveman Code implementation handoff and repository layout rules.

- docs/CAVEMAN_GITNEXUS.md: Cave/Caveman GitNexus MCP configuration and fallback policy.
- .cave/settings.json: Cave/Caveman project-side GitNexus MCP config.
